essay:
Dear Jim,
Haven't seen you for ages. I'm sorry to hear that your parents didn't allow your after plan for biking. Don't be worried, I'd like to give you some constructive suggestions.
To begin with, this is a long time biking plan which lasts for a week. Your parents must concern your safety. You should promise them that you will be safe every day. It will be wiser if you keep contact with them. Let your parents believe that you have the ability to concern yourself.
Plus, if I were you, I would tell them the benefit of biking, such as it can broaden my perspective and it can also enrich my experiences. This is a golden opportunity that can't be missed.
Don't be worried for that. I believe your parents will allow you finally. Look forward to your reply with the least possible delay. Tell me the result.
Yours,
Li Hua

Dear Jim,
Hi! I'm indeed sorry when I learned that you were not allowed to go on your cycling trip. However, don't be too sad on it.
I can really understand your feeling. It indeed feels bad when rejected by our parents. Nevertheless, maybe they thought more about your safety. A week cycling could be a long journey so that they may be very worried about you.
I know that you have always been dreaming about the trip. How about changing a week into 3 days? I suppose you could have a lot of fun although the time is short.
Try to ask them again if 3-day trip is okay. I do hope it can help you. Looking forward to your reply.
Yours,
Li Hua

Dear Jim,
I'm so sorry to hear the bad news. I can understand your feeling because I had similiar experiences like yours. We should not view this experience as a failure but a chance to better your plan so that your parents can allow you to take part in. So don't be sad and make some changes.
I'd like to offer you practical tips to hordle this problem. First, you need to figure out the reson why your parents don't allow your traveling plan. Is there any problems in your plan, including sufty problems? After careful consideration, you need to better your plan and make it more detailed so that your parents can understion it better. After all, have a new talk with them and remember to be calm and patient.
Look forward to your good news.
Yours,
Li Hua

As the final exam is coming, you should prioritize the study and so there necessary things first and spare the time for entertainment. Also, as I know, the climate of your destination isn't suitable for riding.
When it comes to my advice, I think you should prepare for the test first. If you get a high score, your parents may agree with your plan more easily. Try to express your feelings and thoughts with them such as the purpose of the riding instead of argue with them.
Keep positive. I hope my suggestions will help you. Looking forward to your reply.

Dear Jim,
I'm sorry to hear that your plan was not accepted. It is understandable that parents consider in a different way due to the generation and information gap. You might have chance for having another wonderful trip after planning it after standing in your parents' shoes.
Here are some advice for your plan. It's a good idea to shorten your trip to two or three days since your parents might be worried if you leave home for a long time. Given that riding is a little dangerous, you can choose train or bus as an alternative. Most importantly, it's always good to promise that you'll send them messages of where you are, which helps less your parents concerns.
I really hope these tips will help and I'm looking forward to your reply whether you make it.
Yours,
Li Hua
